Cancer-associated fibroblasts condition the ovarian cancer microenvironment and therapeutic responses to antibody-drug conjugates and immunotherapy

High-grade serous ovarian cancer (HGSC) tumors are complex ecosystems where tumor–stromal–immune interactions shape therapeutic responses. Cancer-associated fibroblasts (CAFs) are major regulators in this dynamic crosstalk, yet their spatially distinct functional roles remain poorly understood. Using single-cell and spatial transcriptomics alongside patient-derived ex vivo models, we are mapping how CAF programs are organized at the HGSC tumor–stroma interface (TSI), and how this organization influences therapy sensitivity.

INTRODUCTION

METHODOLOGY

RESULTS

C&H dissociated, raw

Positive enrichment

CONTACT INFORMATION

SELECTED REFERENCES

Nagaraj, A. S. et al. Ex vivo immuno-oncology platform reveals spatial T cell infiltration patterns linked to ATR inhibition responses in High-Grade Serous Ovarian Cancer. Cancer Immunol Res. 2026.
Launonen, I.-M. et al. Chemotherapy induces myeloid-driven spatially confined T cell exhaustion in ovarian cancer. Cancer Cell. 2024
Crouigneau, R. et al. Mimicking and analyzing the tumor microenvironment.

-GeoMX spatial transcriptomics.
-Annotation of tumor-stroma interface (TSI) and (distant) stroma regions of interest (ROIs).
-Cell-type specific deconvolution.
-Pathway enrichment analysis of spatial CAF programs.

Spatial profiling

Mapping of spatial CAF programs

-Projection of spatially-informed CAF signature onto scRNA-seq data.
-Identification of TSI-like and (distant) stroma-like CAFs. 
-Pathway enrichment analysis.

atial CAF programs

-Projection of spatially-informed CAF signature onto scRNA-seq data.
-Identification of TSI-like and (distant) stroma-like CAFs. 
-Pathway enrichment analysis.
-Correlations with immune cell states.

-Establishment of a modular ex vivo model of the HGSC TSI.
-Titration of immunotherapies and antibody-drug conjugates for downstream perturbations. 
-Drug perturbations with selected doses in CAF+ and CAF- models with scRNA-seq and flow cytometry readouts.
